title: Reject rainbow palettes that ignore contrast
summary: AI-generated data displays use full-hue-cycle rainbow palettes where adjacent hues (yellow-green) fail WCAG contrast for overlaid text and differ illegibly from each other.
instruction: Do not use a full-hue-cycle rainbow palette for data, UI elements, or decorative surfaces unless each adjacent pair meets WCAG AA for the content layer above it. Restrict palette hues to at most one-third of the wheel per view.
rationale: Rainbow palettes distribute contrast unevenly: yellow-green adjacent pairs typically score below 1.5:1, while red-blue can exceed 3:1. This forces users to rely on position alone for differentiation and creates inaccessible text contrast. Sequential or luminance-based palettes preserve readability.

violations: A chart legend using red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple fills with white text illegible against yellow and green swatches.
A dashboard status bar using a continuous rainbow gradient with black text at the midpoint where luminance is closest to text color.
controls: A data dashboard using a single-hue sequential palette (light blue to dark blue) for a geographic heatmap with luminance-switching text colors.
A marketing page using a three-color analogous palette (blue-teal-green) with consistent luminance for accessible text overlays.
borderline: A children's education app using full rainbow colors for isolated decorative icons with no overlaid text and a documented educational purpose.

exceptions.description: Data-visualization tools offering rainbow palettes as an explicit user choice with contrast warnings or auto-switching text color per swatch.
examples: Chart libraries with built-in contrast checking
Color-blindness simulation tools where the full spectrum is the instructional material

detector.signalDescription: Detect 3+ CSS color declarations spanning hue intervals >60 degrees apart in the same viewport, suggesting a rainbow palette is in use.
limitations: Cannot distinguish decorative from semantic color usage without element role annotations.
CSS custom properties computed at runtime are invisible to static analysis.
Hue-bin grouping near bin boundaries is approximate.

scenarios: A brand's established color system with 6 hues and careful luminance balancing.
An intentionally maximalist design (music festival site) with a documented broad-palette direction.
mitigations: Flag only when adjacent hue bins are populated AND pairwise contrast falls below 2:1. Allow suppression with a documented design direction.

fixturePlan.description: Marketing page with six cards each using a different hue (red-yellow-green-blue-violet) with white text failing AA on yellow/green.
Dashboard with single-hue sequential blue palette (four luminance steps) with appropriate text colors per step.
Children's app with six pastel hues as isolated icon backgrounds (no text overlay), separated by whitespace.
